item.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and LH side.
The procedures listed below are for the LH side.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector from the skid control sensor.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the speed sensor.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC BRAKE CALIPER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and separate the flexible hose from the shock absorber.
Remove the 2 bolts and separate the rear disc brake caliper assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Use wire or an equivalent tool to keep the brake caliper from hanging down by the flexible hose.

s2.ptxt: Put matchmarks on the rear disc and the axle hub.
Release the parking brake and remove the rear disc.

atten4.ptxt: If the disc cannot be removed easily, turn and press firmly the shoe adjuster until the wheel comes free.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR AXLE HUB AND BEARING ASSEMBLY WITH SKID CONTROL SENSOR

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the skid control sensor connector.
Remove the 4 bolts and hub and bearing assembly.
Mount the rear axle hub in a vise using aluminum plates.

atten3.ptxt: Replace the hub and bearing assembly if it is dropped or receives a strong shock.

s2.ptxt: Using a pin punch and hammer, drive out the 2 pins and remove the 2 attachments from SST.
Using SST and 2 bolts (Diameter: 12 mm, pitch: 1.5 mm), remove the skid control sensor from the hub and bearing assembly.

item.ptxt: Keep the sensor away from magnets.
Pull the skid control sensor off straight, taking care not to make contact with the skid control sensor rotor.
If the speed sensor rotor is damaged or deformed, replace the hub and bearing assembly.
Do not scratch the contact surface between the axle hub and the skid control sensor.
Do not attach foreign matter to the speed sensor rotor and tip.
